title: The Old Man And The C Student

jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Opening credits sequence crash
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase that defines his character - perfect economy and integration but routine by this point in the series

jokes.text: I recommend Moscow, where the American dollar buys seven rubles. - Twelve rubles. - Sixty rubles. 1,000 rubles! I must go!
jokes.setup: Character suggests Moscow based on exchange rate
jokes.punchline: Exchange rate keeps inflating rapidly as he speaks, forcing him to leave
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure with perfect timing and absurd economic reality. Well-earned through character motivation.

jokes.text: I suggest Motown. - Detroit. No, no. Mongolia Town- home of the Motown sound.
jokes.setup: Character suggests 'Motown' which sounds like Detroit
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he means 'Mongolia Town' as birthplace of Motown music
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection joke with cultural absurdity. Good wordplay structure but not exceptional.

jokes.text: giving out medals of beautiful gold... so-so silver and shameful bronze.
jokes.setup: Chairman explains what Olympics are about - medals
jokes.punchline: Describes medals with increasing disdain for lower places
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven humor revealing Olympic committee vanity. Strong quotable phrasing.

jokes.text: Well, I say we don't bring her the torch. I say we bring her... the Olympic Games! - Who is with me? - [ Cricket Chirps ]
jokes.setup: Chairman makes grand gesture to bring Olympics to Springfield
jokes.punchline: Complete silence from committee, indicated by cricket chirps
jokes.explanation: Classic reaction beat comedy with perfect use of sound effects for awkward silence.

jokes.text: Economists predict our city will experience the same boom that Sarajevo enjoyed after the 1984 Games.
jokes.setup: News report about Springfield's expected economic boom
jokes.punchline: References Sarajevo, which was devastated by war after the Olympics
jokes.explanation: Dark historical irony delivered deadpan. Excellent dark comedy writing with real-world knowledge payoff.

jokes.text: Well, actually, I just wrote it for a school assignment. Everyone else wrote to the Backstreet Boys.
jokes.setup: Lisa is praised for her Olympic letter
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was just homework, others wrote to boy bands
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's unintentional achievement vs. her peers' priorities.

jokes.text: The winner will join such other memorable mascots as the Atlanta whatsit and the Montreal vampire.
jokes.setup: News about mascot contest mentions previous Olympic mascots
jokes.punchline: Describes them as forgettable 'whatsit' and absurd 'vampire'
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on unmemorable Olympic mascots. Good observational humor.

jokes.text: No one's gonna jinx you, Homer. In fact, we're rooting for you. - Yeah. Go for the gold, Dad. - Shut up! Shut up! Shut up!
jokes.setup: Family encourages Homer for mascot contest
jokes.punchline: Homer panics about being jinxed by their support
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment showing his superstitious paranoia even about positive support.

jokes.text: You papier-mached my cat?
jokes.setup: Homer presents his Olympic mascot 'Abby the Olympic Tabby'
jokes.punchline: Reveals he literally covered their cat in papier-mache
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd visual gag that's perfectly Homer. Great shock value and quotable line.

jokes.text: Just for the prototype, honey.
jokes.setup: Marge is horrified about the papier-mached cat
jokes.punchline: Homer casually dismisses it as just a prototype
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurd justification. Pure Homer logic in response to wife's horror.

jokes.text: Knowing you always hate my first idea, I prepared a backup.
jokes.setup: Homer acknowledges Marge's reaction to his ideas
jokes.punchline: Shows he actually planned for this rejection
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Homer's self-awareness about his relationship with Marge.

jokes.text: Those aren't the dog's eyes, are they?
jokes.setup: Homer shows second mascot with moving eyes
jokes.punchline: Bart worries Homer used actual dog eyes
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates concern about Homer's crafting methods to darker territory.

jokes.text: And the ends are razor sharp- to protect our nation and its interests.
jokes.setup: Homer describes his spring mascot as fun for the family
jokes.punchline: Reveals it has deadly sharp ends for national defense
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from innocent toy to weapon. Perfect Homer logic mixing nationalism with mascot design.

jokes.text: Hit the road, Lefty. You too, Rizzo. Oh, Cinnamon, don't make this harder than it already is.
jokes.setup: Willie is cleaning up for Olympic committee visit
jokes.punchline: Has emotional goodbye with named rodents he's evicting
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Willie's unexpected tender side with vermin. Good emotional escalation.

jokes.text: Yes. Love that sun, man.
jokes.setup: Olympic committee impressed by 'solar power plant'
jokes.punchline: Homer's hippie-ish casual response to their praise
jokes.explanation: Good Homer moment showing his casual response to formal praise. Character-appropriate reaction.

jokes.text: We will do anything, including but not limited to anything... to make your stay here tolerable. Yes. You'll be completely above the law. Uh, women, guns, cash- whatever you need. It's yours.
jokes.setup: Quimby promises hospitality to Olympic committee
jokes.punchline: Escalates to offering illegal services and immunity
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing corrupt politician desperation. Perfect Quimby character work with dark comedy.

jokes.text: In your face, Patty and Selma! - Well, we still love you, Ciggy.
jokes.setup: Homer gloats that his mascot was chosen
jokes.punchline: Patty and Selma console their cigarette-based mascot
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with visual gag. Reveals their mascot was smoking-themed, fitting their characters.

jokes.text: Mmm! That glue really gives it a pop.
jokes.setup: Homer eating something during ceremony
jokes.punchline: Enjoying the taste of glue from his mascot
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer eating inappropriate things. Good character consistency with food obsession.

jokes.text: I've never wanted a beer worse in my life.
jokes.setup: Children performing 'The Children Are Our Future'
jokes.punchline: Homer's reaction to saccharine children's song
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer reaction to wholesome content. Relatable sentiment delivered with character specificity.

jokes.text: Are you talking to me or the beer? To you, my bubbly, long-necked, beechwood-aged lover.
jokes.setup: Homer says 'I love you honey' while holding beer
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he's talking to the beer, not Marge
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect Homer priorities. Good romantic language applied to beer.

jokes.text: So, uh, you're from Russia, huh? - Da. - You drunk yet? - Da.
jokes.setup: Bart starts comedy routine asking Russian about nationality
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with stereotype about Russian drinking
jokes.explanation: Basic stereotype humor that's somewhat lazy. Gets the job done but relies on cheap cultural assumptions.

jokes.text: Poland, eh? Too easy.
jokes.setup: Bart looks at Polish representative
jokes.punchline: Decides Poland jokes are too obvious to attempt
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about avoiding obvious ethnic humor. Shows restraint while still being funny.

jokes.text: Here's my impression of an East German woman. 'Kees' me, or I'll crush you.
jokes.setup: Bart announces impression of East German woman
jokes.punchline: Combines accent with threatening physical gesture
jokes.explanation: Relies on cultural stereotypes about Eastern European women. Moderately executed but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: He says what we're all thinking. - I'm not thinking that.
jokes.setup: One committee member defends Bart's offensive humor
jokes.punchline: Another immediately contradicts him
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing divided response to offensive humor. Clean structure and timing.

jokes.text: Hey, Swiss miss. There's no missin' you, babe. Lay off the cocoa.
jokes.setup: Bart addresses Swiss representative
jokes.punchline: Makes pun on Swiss Miss cocoa brand and suggests she's overweight
jokes.explanation: Weak pun combined with body-shaming humor. Structure works but content is lazy.

jokes.text: If there was a medal for horrible audiences, you'd get the gold. Peace out.
jokes.setup: Bart addresses unresponsive Olympic committee
jokes.punchline: Insults them using Olympic medal metaphor, then exits casually
jokes.explanation: Good closer that uses Olympic theme against the committee. Shows Bart's attitude when bombing.

jokes.text: Well, it really did seem funny in rehearsal. And he didn't even get to his Ubangi routine.
jokes.setup: Skinner explains why he allowed Bart's offensive act
jokes.punchline: Reveals there was even more offensive material planned
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Skinner's poor judgment and that things could have been worse.

jokes.text: Well, I was born in Queens, went to Ball State... then made the move to Intercourse, Pennsylvania. Uh, why do you ask?
jokes.setup: Chalmers gives his biographical background
jokes.punchline: Mentions living in actual town with sexual name
jokes.explanation: Solid setup using real Pennsylvania town name. Works because character is unaware of why it's funny.

jokes.text: Let's see you make a joke out of that, Mr. Smart Guy.
jokes.setup: Chalmers mentions his vacation at Lake Titicaca
jokes.punchline: Challenges audience/Bart to make fun of the name
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-moment where character dares audience to laugh at silly place name. Good self-awareness.

jokes.text: All right, gang. Shirts and skins. Let's hustle. - [ Gunshot ] [ Tires Screeching ]
jokes.setup: Martin enthusiastically starts basketball program
jokes.punchline: Immediate gunshot and tires screeching, suggesting violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect timing. Sound effects create dark comedy without showing violence.

jokes.text: Ow! I pricked myself. Well, just keep working. You'll prick yourself with the antidote sooner or later.
jokes.setup: Milhouse hurts himself on medical waste
jokes.punchline: Skinner suggests he'll randomly find antidote in more medical waste
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy with absurd medical logic. Shows callous adult authority figure.

jokes.text: The fireworks, candy and puppy dog store? No, no, no. Next to it.
jokes.setup: Bart excited about apparent paradise workplace
jokes.punchline: Skinner clarifies it's the nursing home next door
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection structure. Creates contrast between fantasy and dreary reality.

jokes.text: Settle a bet. Boil or mole?
jokes.setup: Bart enters nursing home
jokes.punchline: Elderly resident asks him to identify skin growth
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor establishing the uncomfortable nursing home environment immediately.

jokes.text: Just breathe through your mouth... and don't ask how they're feeling.
jokes.setup: Nurse gives Bart orientation to nursing home
jokes.punchline: Practical advice about avoiding unpleasant realities
jokes.explanation: Dark but practical humor about nursing home realities. Good institutional cynicism.

jokes.text: Let's pull his skin.
jokes.setup: Elderly residents encounter young Bart
jokes.punchline: Want to pull his skin like a curiosity
jokes.explanation: Creepy but funny reversal - usually kids want to touch old people's skin, here it's reversed.

jokes.text: Oh, be a dear and snap my support hose to my girdle.
jokes.setup: Elderly woman needs help with undergarments
jokes.punchline: Asks young boy to help with intimate clothing adjustment
jokes.explanation: Standard nursing home awkwardness. Gets discomfort laugh but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: You sunk my battleship. - You sunk my battleship.
jokes.setup: Residents playing games during recreation time
jokes.punchline: Both say same line, suggesting confusion or limited game options
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about elderly confusion or repetitive activities. Functional but not standout.

jokes.text: Oh! I got a bingo! What do I win? - A banana. - A whole one? - Yep.
jokes.setup: Resident wins bingo and asks about prize
jokes.punchline: Prize is single banana, resident excited about 'whole' one
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff showing institutional cheapness and residents' low expectations. Well-structured.

jokes.text: That's the prize? A banana? Their natural mushiness prevents choking and promotes regularity.
jokes.setup: Bart questions pathetic bingo prize
jokes.punchline: Nurse gives practical medical justification for banana choice
jokes.explanation: Good institutional humor with medical reasoning for seemingly cheap prizes. Shows systemic thinking.

jokes.text: They're not babies, Lisa. Give 'em something fun- like cigars or booze. We tried giving them eggnog at Christmas... but it led to widespread 'de-shawling.'
jokes.setup: Bart suggests better prizes, nurse explains why not
jokes.punchline: Eggnog caused elderly to remove their shawls en masse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with invented term 'de-shawling.' Perfect institutional horror story with absurd specific detail.

jokes.text: While they sleep, we suck up excess dirt and crumbs. Here you go. Make sure you get into every crevice.
jokes.setup: Nurse explains nap time routine
jokes.punchline: They vacuum elderly residents while they sleep
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark institutional horror. Treating humans like furniture that needs cleaning. Original and disturbing.

jokes.text: Don't play with the faces.
jokes.setup: Bart is vacuuming sleeping elderly residents
jokes.punchline: Nurse casually warns him not to mess with their faces
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of horrific instruction. Excellent economy - maximum disturbing impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: Yeah, but I should have no problem selling a thousand springs. - To who? - Idiots.
jokes.setup: Homer confident about selling rejected Olympic springs
jokes.punchline: Plans to target idiots as customers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - honest about his business model. Good structure with Marge's setup question.

jokes.text: Frankly, my dear- I love you. Let's remarry.
jokes.setup: Residents watching censored 'Gone with the Wind'
jokes.punchline: Famous dismissive line changed to romantic reconciliation
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection using famous movie line. Good satire of over-sanitizing content for elderly.

jokes.text: They cut out the best word. Didn't that movie used to have a war in it?
jokes.setup: Residents notice censorship of profanity
jokes.punchline: Realize entire Civil War was edited out
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from word censorship to entire plot removal. Brilliant observational humor about sanitization.

jokes.text: Boring! Come on, Bart. We don't want to overstimulate these people. They just had pudding.
jokes.setup: Bart tries to liven up relaxation exercise
jokes.punchline: Nurse warns about overstimulation after pudding consumption
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic treating pudding as major stimulation. Shows institutional overcaution.

jokes.text: Your little smart-mouthed boy cost this town the Olympics.
jokes.setup: Agnes recognizes Homer at door
jokes.punchline: Blames Bart for Olympic loss
jokes.explanation: Good callback connecting spring sales to Olympic controversy. Solid character work for Agnes.

jokes.text: Boy, Lenny, you sure look hungry. Have some nuts. - Hey, thanks. - Ow! My eye! Ow! Ow! Ow!
jokes.setup: Homer offers Lenny nuts in friendly gesture
jokes.punchline: Spring-loaded nuts attack Lenny's eye
jokes.explanation: Classic slapstick with spring mechanism. Good setup but fairly standard physical comedy execution.

jokes.text: Boy, Moe, you sure look angry. Here. Have some nuts. - Hey, thanks. Ow! God! My eye!
jokes.setup: Homer repeats exact same spring nut trick on Moe
jokes.punchline: Same eye injury occurs to different victim
jokes.explanation: Weak repetition of previous gag. Gets some value from repetition structure but lacks originality.

jokes.text: Ahoy, mateys! It's me- Long Bart Silver. And I'm gonna rip you a new I.V. hole.
jokes.setup: Bart disrupts seniors' peaceful boat fantasy as pirate
jokes.punchline: Makes medical pun combining pirate threat with I.V. terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever pun adapting pirate threat to nursing home context. Good character work showing Bart's disruptive nature.

jokes.text: These people need to ride motorcycles and play rockin' electric guitars... like the old people on TV.
jokes.setup: Bart explains his disruptive behavior
jokes.punchline: References unrealistic TV portrayals of active elderly
jokes.explanation: Good media satire about unrealistic elderly representation. Shows Bart's pop culture influence on worldview.

jokes.text: But when those pirates boarded... I swallowed my wedding ring for safekeeping. Get me some ipecac. I'd like to expunge it.
jokes.setup: Resident took Bart's pirate fantasy seriously
jokes.punchline: Swallowed valuable jewelry and wants to vomit it up
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing elderly confused reality with fantasy. Medical terminology makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, I don't live here. I'm just dropping off Indian casino pamphlets. Vote 'yes' on Prop 217.
jokes.setup: Bart asks Chief if he wants to escape nursing home
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's just visiting to distribute gambling pamphlets
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character work. Shows Wiggum's side job promoting questionable causes to elderly.

jokes.text: You know, the door was open, Chief Break Everything.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum apparently broke something on his way out
jokes.punchline: Staff member gives him sarcastic nickname
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Wiggum's clumsiness. Nickname is creative and fits the character.

jokes.text: You don't wanna stay in a place where they vacuum you while you sleep.
jokes.setup: Bart convinces seniors to escape with him
jokes.punchline: References the horrific vacuuming practice as motivation
jokes.explanation: Good callback that recontextualizes earlier horror as motivation. Shows Bart's persuasive skills.

jokes.text: Today's grass is far sharper than the grass in my day.
jokes.setup: Elderly residents fall on grass during their escape adventure
jokes.punchline: Blames modern grass quality for discomfort
jokes.explanation: Perfect elderly character humor. Blames generational change for everything, even grass quality.

jokes.text: Arr! Not a looker among 'em.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain sees boatload of elderly nursing home residents
jokes.punchline: Dismisses their attractiveness entirely
jokes.explanation: Good character work for Sea Captain's blunt personality. Economical delivery of harsh observation.

jokes.text: What'd he say? Put on our tuxedos? I want some taquitos.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain shouts 'Damn the torpedoes'
jokes.punchline: Elderly mishear as 'tuxedos' and 'taquitos'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay based on elderly hearing problems. Shows character-appropriate confusion with food obsession.

jokes.text: How much would you pay for a self-flipping hamburger pan? Nothing. Don't answer yet.
jokes.setup: Homer doing infomercial pitch for kitchen gadget
jokes.punchline: Marge's immediate rejection followed by Homer ignoring her answer
jokes.explanation: Good infomercial parody - Homer's persistence despite obvious rejection is perfect

jokes.text: Some second-degree burns. But some first-class burgers.
jokes.setup: Homer injured by spring-loaded hamburger flipper
jokes.punchline: Downplays injury while praising burger quality
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay with second-degree/first-class. Perfect Homer prioritizing food over safety.

jokes.text: 'D' it up, Marge. I'm goin' to the hole.
jokes.setup: Homer demonstrates baby bouncer with Maggie
jokes.punchline: Uses basketball terminology while endangering baby
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Homer's inappropriate sports references during dangerous parenting.

jokes.text: I haven't felt this relaxed and carefree... since I was watch commander at Pearl Harbor.
jokes.setup: Grampa enjoying boat trip, feels relaxed
jokes.punchline: Compares to being on duty during Pearl Harbor attack
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor connecting relaxation to military disaster. Perfect Grampa character work with historical irony.

jokes.text: They don't even seem to care that it's medication time. - Medication time? Hot dog! - Gimme, gimme, gimme!
jokes.setup: Lisa notes seniors ignoring their schedule
jokes.punchline: Mere mention of medication causes frenzied excitement
jokes.explanation: Good irony showing seniors' drug dependency despite supposed freedom. Well-structured revelation.

jokes.text: Let's play bingo. - Yeah! Let's play bingo. - You sunk my battleship.
jokes.setup: Bart offers freedom from nursing home activities
jokes.punchline: Seniors immediately want to play the same games anyway
jokes.explanation: Good irony about creatures of habit. Shows the futility of Bart's rebellion against routine.

jokes.text: Gotta do somethin' till bingo.
jokes.setup: Bart asks why they followed him if they like boring stuff
jokes.punchline: Admits it was just to kill time until next bingo game
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery revealing elderly priorities. Excellent economy and character work.

jokes.text: Oh. Two glass eyes.
jokes.setup: Asked how he missed the huge boat collision
jokes.punchline: Reveals he has glass eyes with sound effect
jokes.explanation: Good physical revelation with sound effect. Explains boat captain's poor navigation skills absurdly.

jokes.text: I'm supposed to die in a foolish motorcycle stunt at the age of 15.
jokes.setup: Bart protests that he's not supposed to die now
jokes.punchline: Has specific prediction for his own death by motorcycle
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect Bart character work. Shows his reckless nature and fatalistic attitude.

jokes.text: Don't worry. I'll save you the Jack La Lanne way!
jokes.setup: Everyone drowning in boat disaster
jokes.punchline: Celebrity fitness guru appears to help with fitness-based rescue
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity cameo that fits the elderly theme. Absurd but character-appropriate rescue method.

jokes.text: Sure, Bart's responsible for our deaths. But he gave us the most fun we had in 20 years.
jokes.setup: Residents blame Bart for their drowning predicament
jokes.punchline: Admits it was still their most enjoyable experience in decades
jokes.explanation: Excellent bittersweet humor about nursing home life quality. Perfect character work showing elderly perspective.

jokes.text: Ah. What a shame. Not a looker in the bunch.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain sees the now-rescued elderly residents
jokes.punchline: Repeats his earlier dismissive comment about their appearance
jokes.explanation: Simple callback that shows character consistency. Gets mild value from repetition but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: How low can I go!
jokes.setup: Bart brings limbo stick to nursing home
jokes.punchline: Elderly resident enthusiastically participates in limbo
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing elderly person's unexpected enthusiasm. Decent character work but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Hey. Stop shakin' the harness. How low can I go! [Water Splashes]
jokes.setup: Elderly person in rescue harness
jokes.punchline: Treating rescue equipment like limbo stick, falls in water
jokes.explanation: Good callback - elderly person still thinking about limbo even during rescue
